seoTitle: Lake Haven vs Lake Heights — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lake Haven and Lake Heights. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lake Haven vs Lake Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lake Haven and Lake Heights.
heading: Lake Haven vs Lake Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lake Haven, New South Wales and Lake Heights, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lake Heights has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Lake Haven (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lake Haven or Lake Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lake Haven has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Lake Heights scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lake Haven scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Lake Heights scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lake Haven or Lake Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lake Heights has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
